load.__returns: Returns the corresponding Lua value or a nil value in case of error.
load.__example:
local net = require "net"
local json = require "json"

print("Downloading dummy json file...")
local http = await(net.Http("https://microsoftedge.github.io"):download("/Demos/json-dummy-data/64KB.json"))

-- Checks that everything went right during download
if http.response.file.exists then
    -- load users table from the download JSON file
    local users = json.load(http.response.file)
    for user in each(users) do
        print(user.name)
    end
else
    error("Failed to load JSON file")
end

__params.__description: A File object or a filename string, representing the file whose content will be decoded from JSON.

load.__description: Load and decode a file containing JSON data.
load.__tinydesc: Load and decode a file containing JSON data

decode.__returns: Returns the corresponding Lua value or a nil value in case of error.
decode.__example:
local net = require "net" 
local json = require "json"

-- use the free IP-API localization web API
local url = "http://ip-api.com"
local client = net.Http(url)

-- wait for a GET request to terminate
await(client:get("/json/"))

-- decode the response as a JSON string or throw an error 
local data = json.decode(client.response and client.response.content or error(net.error))

-- use the needed response fields
print("You are located in "..data.country.." near "..data.city)
decode.__description:
Decode a JSON string to a Lua value, following those rules :
JSON Lua value type
"string" "string"
number number
"true" / "false" boolean
object {} table
array [] table (integer keys)
null "null"
decode.__tinydesc: Generate a Lua value from a JSON string

save.__returns: Returns true or false in case of error.

__params.__description: A File object or a filename string, representing the file to save the JSON data.

save.__description: Encode a Lua value to a JSON representation string and save it to a file.
save.__tinydesc: Encode a Lua value to a JSON representation string and save it to a file

encode.__returns: Returns the encoded JSON string or a nil value in case of error.
encode.__example:
-- use the json binary module
-- must be in the same folder as this Lua script
-- or in the 'modules' folder of your LuaRT installation path
local json = require "json"

-- Lua array (integer only indexed table)
local array = { 1, 2, 3, "Hello !" }

-- prints the JSON representation of the array value
-- should output [1,2,3,"Hello !"]

print(json.encode(array))
encode.__description:
Encode any Lua value in a JSON representation string, following those rules :
Lua value type JSON
"string" "string"
number number
boolean "true" / "false"
table object {}
table (integer keys) array []
"null" null
object Not supported (converted to JSON object {})
thread Not supported (converted to "string")
userdata Not supported (converted to "string")
encode.__tinydesc: Encode a Lua value to a JSON representation string

__description: The json module allows for encoding and decoding JSON data
__tinydesc: Parses and generates JSON data for easy data exchange
